claim: Naive RAG falls victim to 'satisfaction of search' - agents stop looking after finding first result that seems relevant, missing better approaches
actionable: Implement exhaustive retrieval strategies in RAG systems that don't stop at first results; use reranking to evaluate all candidates before selecting
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked

claim: Simply providing MCPs and tools to agents doesn't solve the context problem - access is not understanding; agents need reasoning about what information matters
actionable: When building agentic systems, prioritize building a context engine that reasons about what matters, not just providing tool access
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked

claim: A context engine should use social graphs as a pivot point to personalize what information matters (e.g., PR history, team relationships, CTO authority in Slack)
actionable: Build social graphs from your org (PRs, code ownership, Slack conversations, team structure) and use them to prioritize relevant context for agents
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked

claim: A million-token context window sitting empty is not helpful - context engines need to reason about and compress information into exactly what agents need
actionable: Rather than trying to fit everything into a large context window, build systems that intelligently select and compress only relevant information
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked

claim: Context engines should resolve conflicts between sources (e.g., code vs Slack conversations) by using social signals like CTO authority
actionable: When your code base and documentation disagree, use organizational hierarchy and expertise signals to determine which is most trustworthy
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked

claim: Code that compiles but breaks in production is a result of agents missing architectural patterns, factory patterns, and fallback infrastructure from across the codebase
actionable: Use a context engine to preload agents with knowledge of architectural patterns and infrastructure patterns before they start writing code
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked

claim: Caching 'good answers' from context engines is harmful because information changes over time - a correct answer today is stale tomorrow
actionable: Never cache context engine responses for agents; always compute fresh to ensure accuracy with current code, configs, and decisions
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked

claim: Expert graphs showing code ownership, PR patterns, and team relationships enable agents to ask the right questions and find the right information
actionable: Generate expert graphs from your repository's commit history, PR reviews, and team structure to help agents understand who knows what
source_title: Stop babysitting your agents... — Brandon Waselnuk, Unblocked
